target.name: Roam Robotics
target.founder: Tim Swift, Mallory Ulaszek, Saul Griffith

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Roam Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $16.4M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable, all operating in the robotics and manufacturing sector at Series A or Series B stages. Key private peers include 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, with valuations ranging from $350M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $16.4M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $33M. Peer-median analysis across the six most comparable same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated range spans $28M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median implied valuation of $554M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects meaningful uncertainty about where Roam will ultimately land within the sector's maturation curve. Key variables affecting valuation placement include technology differentiation relative to the peer cohort, path to revenue monetization, and whether the company trajectories toward the lower-median or upper-quartile outcomes observed in the comparable set.
